info.title: DataForSEO API
info.description: DataForSEO API endpoints exposed through the AIsa unified gateway.

post.summary: AI Keyword Data Keyword Search Volume
post.description: Returns estimated AI-tool search-volume data for the target keywords in a given location and language.

version.description: The API version that produced this response.

status_code.description: Overall status code of the response. Inspect it to detect request-level errors.

status_message.description: Human-readable message accompanying the overall status code.

time.description: Total execution time of the request, in seconds.

cost.description: Total cost of all tasks in this response, in USD.

tasks_count.description: Number of tasks in the `tasks` array.

tasks_error.description: Number of tasks in the `tasks` array that returned an error.

tasks.description: List of tasks created for this request.

tasks.id.description: Unique task identifier in UUID format.

tasks.status_code.description: Status code of this individual task. Use it to tell whether the task itself succeeded.

tasks.status_message.description: Human-readable message for this task's status.

tasks.time.description: Execution time of this task, in seconds.

tasks.cost.description: Cost of this task, in USD.

tasks.result_count.description: Number of elements in this task's `result` array.

tasks.path.description: API route segments of the endpoint that handled this task.

tasks.data.description: Echo of the parameters you supplied for this task.

tasks.result.description: Results produced by this task.

tasks.result.location_code.description: Location code echoed from the request.

tasks.result.language_code.description: Language code echoed from the request.

tasks.result.items_count.description: Number of elements in this result's `items` array.

tasks.result.items.description: The data records returned for this result.

tasks.result.items.keyword.description: The keyword this record refers to.

tasks.result.items.ai_search_volume.description: Current AI search-volume rate for the keyword.

tasks.result.items.ai_monthly_searches.description: Per-month AI search-volume history for the keyword.

tasks.result.items.ai_monthly_searches.y.description: Year of the monthly data point.

tasks.result.items.ai_monthly_searches.a.description: AI search-volume rate for that month.

keywords.description: Target keywords whose AI search-volume data you want to retrieve. Each entry is processed as a separate keyword.

location_name.description: Full location name (e.g. `United States`). Provide this or `location_code`; the available values come from the corresponding Locations endpoint.

location_code.description: Numeric location identifier. Provide this or `location_name`; the available values come from the corresponding Locations endpoint.

language_name.description: Full language name (e.g. `English`). Provide this or `language_code`; the available values come from the corresponding Languages endpoint.

language_code.description: Language code (e.g. `en`). Provide this or `language_name`; the available values come from the corresponding Languages endpoint.

tag.description: Your own label for the task, echoed back in the response so you can match results to requests.

version.description: The API version that produced this response.

status_code.description: Overall status code of the response. Inspect it to detect request-level errors.

status_message.description: Human-readable message accompanying the overall status code.

tasks.time.description: Execution time of this task, in seconds.

tasks.cost.description: Cost of this task, in USD.

tasks.result_count.description: Number of elements in this task's `result` array.

tasks.data.description: Echo of the parameters you supplied for this task.

tasks.result.description: Results produced by this task.

tasks.result.model_name.description: Name of the AI model that produced the result.

tasks.result.input_tokens.description: Total number of tokens in the input.

tasks.result.output_tokens.description: Total number of tokens generated in the response.

tasks.result.reasoning_tokens.description: Number of tokens used to produce the reasoning content.

tasks.result.web_search.description: Whether web search was used to produce this result.

tasks.result.money_spent.description: Amount charged by the third-party AI provider for the tokens, in USD.

tasks.result.datetime.description: When the result was produced, in UTC.

tasks.result.items.description: The data records returned for this result.

tasks.result.items.reasoning.description: The reasoning element of the response (reasoning models only).

tasks.result.items.reasoning.type.description: Element type marker for the reasoning element.

tasks.result.items.reasoning.sections.description: The reasoning-chain sections generated by the model.

tasks.result.items.reasoning.sections.ty.description: Element type marker for the reasoning section.

tasks.result.items.reasoning.sections.te.description: Text summarizing this part of the model's thought process.

tasks.result.items.message.description: The message element of the response.

tasks.result.items.message.type.description: Element type marker for the message element.

tasks.result.items.message.sections.description: The content sections that make up the AI message.

tasks.result.items.message.sections.type.description: Element type marker for the content section.

tasks.result.items.message.sections.anno.description: References cited to generate the response; null when web search was not used and may be empty.
Domain name or title of the cited source.

tasks.result.fan_out_queries.description: Related search queries derived from the main query to broaden the response.

user_prompt.description: The question or instruction sent to the AI model.

model_name.description: Which AI model to query. Combines the base model name and version; the available values come from the corresponding Models endpoint.

max_output_tokens.description: Upper bound on the number of tokens the model may generate in its reply. Limits response length and cost.

temperature.description: Sampling randomness: higher values make the output more varied, lower values make it more focused and deterministic.

top_p.description: Nucleus-sampling threshold controlling response diversity by limiting which tokens are eligible for selection.

web_search.description: Whether the model may access and cite current web information when answering. When disabled, the model answers from its training knowledge only.

force_web_search.description: Whether to force the model to perform a web search rather than leaving it optional. Requires web search to be enabled where that toggle exists.

web_search_country_iso_code.description: ISO country code used to localize web-search results.

web_search_city.description: City name used to localize web-search results. Requires `web_search_country_iso_code` to be set.

system_message.description: Instructions that shape the AI's role, tone, or behavior, applied before the user prompt.

message_chain.description: Prior conversation turns supplied as context. Each element is a message object carrying a role and its content.

tag.description: Your own label for the task, echoed back in the response so you can match results to requests.

tasks.result.competition.description: Relative paid-search competition level for the keyword; null when no data is available.

tasks.result.cpc.description: Average historical cost-per-click in USD for the keyword; null when no data is available.

tasks.result.search_volume.description: Approximate monthly average number of searches for the keyword under the requested targeting.

tasks.result.categories.description: Product/service categories for the keyword. Legacy field that is always null.

tasks.result.monthly_searches.description: Per-month search-volume history for the keyword over the trailing window.

tasks.result.monthly_searches.year.description: Year of this monthly data point.

tasks.result.monthly_searches.month.description: Month of this monthly data point.

tasks.result.monthly_searches.search_vol.description: Average search volume for that month.

keywords.description: Seed keywords to analyze; pass them as an array of strings. Capping and character rules are enforced by the endpoint, and non-Latin characters bias results toward the regions where those characters are used.

location_name.description: Full location name used for geo targeting (e.g. country, region, city). Mutually replaceable with location_code; omit both to get global results. Look up valid names via the matching locations_and_languages / locations endpoint.

location_code.description: Numeric location identifier used for geo targeting; an alternative to location_name. Omit both to get global results. Data is reported for the country the code belongs to. Look up valid codes via the matching locations endpoint.

location_coordinate.description: GPS-based geo target given as `latitude,longitude,radius_km`. An alternative to location_name / location_code; data is reported for the country the coordinates fall in.

language_name.description: Full search-engine language name (e.g. English) for the query. Mutually replaceable with language_code. Look up valid names via the matching locations_and_languages endpoint.

language_code.description: Search-engine language code (e.g. en) for the query. Mutually replaceable with language_name. Look up valid codes via the matching locations_and_languages endpoint.

sort_by.description: Metric used to order the returned keyword rows in descending order (e.g. by search volume, CPC, competition, or relevance).

keywords_negative.description: Negative keyword terms to exclude from the result set. Matching items are dropped from the response and the terms are lowercased before filtering.

device.description: Restricts the returned metrics to a single device type. Leave unset to aggregate across all devices.

date_from.description: Start of the time window for historical/trend data, in `yyyy-mm-dd`. Endpoint-specific minimum dates apply; omit to default to the start of the comparable prior period.

date_to.description: End of the time window for historical/trend data, in `yyyy-mm-dd`. Omit to default to the current date.

search_partners.description: Whether to include syndicated search-partner networks in addition to the primary engine. Enable to widen reach across partner sites.

tag.description: Your own label for the task, echoed back in the response so you can correlate results with the request that produced them.

status_code.description: Overall response status code for the request; check it before reading the payload and design handling for error conditions.

status_message.description: Human-readable message accompanying the overall status code.

time.description: Server-side processing time for the request, in seconds.

cost.description: Total billed cost of this response across all tasks, in USD.

tasks_count.description: Number of task entries contained in the tasks array.

tasks_error.description: How many of the tasks finished with an error; if non-zero, inspect each task's own status fields.

tasks.description: List of task results; one entry per task created by the request.

tasks.id.description: Unique identifier (UUID) of this task in the provider's system.

tasks.status_code.description: Per-task status code; check it to tell whether this individual task succeeded.

tasks.status_message.description: Human-readable message for this task's status code.

tasks.time.description: Processing time for this individual task, in seconds.

tasks.cost.description: Billed cost of this individual task, in USD.

tasks.result_count.description: Number of entries in this task's result array.

tasks.path.description: URL path segments identifying the endpoint that handled this task.

tasks.data.description: Echo of the parameters you submitted in the request, returned for traceability.

tasks.result.description: Array carrying the actual keyword/trend data produced for this task; drill into its items for per-keyword figures.

tasks.result.keywords.description: The keywords submitted in the request that this result corresponds to.

tasks.result.type.description: Search-engine/data type echoed from the request.

tasks.result.location_code.description: Location code echoed from the request; null when no data is available.

tasks.result.language_code.description: Language code echoed from the request; null when no data is available.

tasks.result.datetime.description: UTC timestamp when this result was produced.

tasks.result.items_count.description: Number of entries in this result's items array.

tasks.result.items.description: Detailed per-keyword (or per-element) data rows for this result.

tasks.result.items.position.description: Ordinal position of this element within the items array.

tasks.result.items.type.description: Element type of this item row, indicating which kind of data block it carries.

tasks.result.items.keywords.description: Keywords that the demography and comparison data in this item are based on.

tasks.result.items.interests.description: Subregional keyword popularity data for each specified term.

tasks.result.items.interests.keyword.description: Keyword the subregional values are based on.

tasks.result.items.interests.values.description: Relative keyword popularity broken down by country or region.

tasks.result.items.interests.values.geo_.description: Location identifier; use it to match results back to the location parameters in your request.
Location name; use it to match results back to the location parameters in your request.

tasks.result.items.interests.values.valu.description: Location-specific relative keyword popularity over the requested time range.

tasks.result.items.interests_comparison.description: Comparison of subregional popularity across the specified terms; null when only one keyword was supplied.

tasks.result.items.interests_comparison..description: Per-location popularity values normalized relative to the maximum within each region.
Location identifier; use it to match results back to your request's location parameters.
Location name; use it to match results back to your request's location parameters.
Relative popularity within the given location over the requested time range.
Per-location popularity values normalized relative to the maximum across all locations.

Location name; use it to match results back to your request's location parameters.
Location-specific relative popularity, normalized across all locations.

keywords.description: Seed keywords to analyze; pass them as an array of strings. Capping and character rules are enforced by the endpoint, and non-Latin characters bias results toward the regions where those characters are used.

location_name.description: Full location name used for geo targeting (e.g. country, region, city). Mutually replaceable with location_code; omit both to get global results. Look up valid names via the matching locations_and_languages / locations endpoint.

location_code.description: Numeric location identifier used for geo targeting; an alternative to location_name. Omit both to get global results. Data is reported for the country the code belongs to. Look up valid codes via the matching locations endpoint.

type.description: Trends data category to query (e.g. web, news, ecommerce). Selects which corpus the popularity figures are drawn from.

date_from.description: Start of the time window for historical/trend data, in `yyyy-mm-dd`. Endpoint-specific minimum dates apply; omit to default to the start of the comparable prior period.

date_to.description: End of the time window for historical/trend data, in `yyyy-mm-dd`. Omit to default to the current date.

time_range.description: Preset relative time window for trend data, used as a shortcut instead of date_from/date_to. Ignored if explicit dates are supplied.

tag.description: Your own label for the task, echoed back in the response so you can correlate results with the request that produced them.

keywords.description: Seed keywords to analyze; pass them as an array of strings. Capping and character rules are enforced by the endpoint, and non-Latin characters bias results toward the regions where those characters are used.

bid.description: Hypothetical max bid in USD used to forecast ad traffic. Drives the estimated impressions, clicks, and spend figures returned.

match.description: Keyword match type that controls how strictly user queries must match your keyword (aggregate / broad / phrase / exact), shaping which queries contribute to the metrics.

search_partners.description: Whether to include syndicated search-partner networks in addition to the primary engine. Enable to widen reach across partner sites.

location_name.description: Full location name used for geo targeting (e.g. country, region, city). Mutually replaceable with location_code; omit both to get global results. Look up valid names via the matching locations_and_languages / locations endpoint.

location_code.description: Numeric location identifier used for geo targeting; an alternative to location_name. Omit both to get global results. Data is reported for the country the code belongs to. Look up valid codes via the matching locations endpoint.

location_coordinate.description: GPS-based geo target given as `latitude,longitude,radius_km`. An alternative to location_name / location_code; data is reported for the country the coordinates fall in.

language_name.description: Full search-engine language name (e.g. English) for the query. Mutually replaceable with language_code. Look up valid names via the matching locations_and_languages endpoint.

language_code.description: Search-engine language code (e.g. en) for the query. Mutually replaceable with language_name. Look up valid codes via the matching locations_and_languages endpoint.

date_from.description: Start of the time window for historical/trend data, in `yyyy-mm-dd`. Endpoint-specific minimum dates apply; omit to default to the start of the comparable prior period.

date_to.description: End of the time window for historical/trend data, in `yyyy-mm-dd`. Omit to default to the current date.

date_interval.description: Forecasting horizon for ad-traffic estimates (e.g. next week/month/quarter), used as a shortcut instead of date_from/date_to.

sort_by.description: Metric used to order the returned keyword rows in descending order (e.g. by search volume, CPC, competition, or relevance).

postback_url.description: Endpoint that receives a POST callback with the gzipped task results once the task finishes. Supports the `$id` and `$tag` placeholders, which are substituted before the callback is sent.

pingback_url.description: Endpoint that receives a lightweight GET ping when the task completes, signalling you to fetch the results. Supports the `$id` and `$tag` placeholders, substituted before the ping is sent.

tag.description: Your own label for the task, echoed back in the response so you can correlate results with the request that produced them.

asins.description: Target products' ASINs to pull ranking data for in one batch; obtain each ASIN from the Amazon Products endpoint and supply them in uppercase.

limit.description: Caps how many records are returned in the results array.

intersection_mode.description: How the supplied ASINs are matched against keywords: intersect keeps only keywords where every ASIN co-ranks, while union keeps keywords where any of them ranks.

filters.description:

target.description: Target domain or subdomain to analyze; specify it without the https:// scheme and without the www. prefix.

location_name.description: Geographic target given by its full location name; supply either this or location_code. Look up valid names via the locations_and_languages endpoint.

location_code.description: Geographic target given by its numeric location code; supply either this or location_name. Look up valid codes via the locations_and_languages endpoint.

language_name.description: Target language given by its full name; supply either this or language_code. Look up valid names via the locations_and_languages endpoint.

language_code.description: Target language given by its short code; supply either this or language_name. Look up valid codes via the locations_and_languages endpoint.

include_subcategories.description: When true, results also span the subcategories of matched categories; when false, only top-level categories are considered.

include_clickstream_data.description: When true, enriches the response with clickstream-based metrics (such as clickstream search volume and gender/age distributions). Leaving it off keeps those fields empty.

historical_serp_mode.description: Controls which SERPs are counted: live keeps only SERPs where the target currently ranks, while lost keeps SERPs where it ranked before but no longer does.

item_types.description: Restricts results to the listed SERP result types; the first type listed also determines the default ordering of results.

filters.description:
Conditions that narrow the result set; combine clauses with the and / or logical operators. Reference fields by their response dotpath (e.g. metrics.organic.pos_1).
[⚠️Note:源码描述的运算符与最大条件数在各接口间不一致且部分符号被渲染截断，具体可用运算符与上限请以原生 description 为准，待研发确认。]

order_by.description: Sorting rules over the same fields usable in filters; each rule pairs a field with an asc or desc direction.

limit.description: Caps how many records are returned in the results array.

offset.description: Skips this many leading records in the results array, for paging through results.

tag.description: Free-form label you attach to the task so you can match it to its result later; echoed back in the response data object.

correlate.description: When enabled, aligns the returned series with previously obtained datasets to smooth over inconsistencies introduced by database changes.

ignore_synonyms.description: When true, collapses highly similar keywords and returns only the core keyword of each cluster.

tag.description: Free-form label you attach to the task so you can match it to its result later; echoed back in the response data object.

post.description: Returns keyword ideas relevant to the seed keywords by drawing on the categories the seeds belong to, each enriched with Google Ads metrics, SERP data, and search history, to expand a topic into a broader keyword set.

keywords.description: Target keywords to process in one request; supplied in UTF-8 and converted to lowercase before matching.

location_name.description: Geographic target given by its full location name; supply either this or location_code. Look up valid names via the locations_and_languages endpoint.

location_code.description: Geographic target given by its numeric location code; supply either this or location_name. Look up valid codes via the locations_and_languages endpoint.

language_name.description: Target language given by its full name; supply either this or language_code. Look up valid names via the locations_and_languages endpoint.

language_code.description: Target language given by its short code; supply either this or language_name. Look up valid codes via the locations_and_languages endpoint.

closely_variants.description: Selects the match algorithm: true uses phrase-match, false uses broad-match, which widens the returned keyword set.

ignore_synonyms.description: When true, collapses highly similar keywords and returns only the core keyword of each cluster.

include_serp_info.description: When true, attaches a serp_info block per keyword with SERP-level data such as result count, a check URL, and the SERP feature types present.

include_clickstream_data.description: When true, enriches the response with clickstream-based metrics (such as clickstream search volume and gender/age distributions). Leaving it off keeps those fields empty.

limit.description: Caps how many records are returned in the results array.

offset.description: Skips this many leading records in the results array, for paging through results.

offset_token.description: Pagination cursor for fetching subsequent pages beyond the offset limit; pass back the offset_token value returned in the previous response. Each token is single-use for the next page.

filters.description:
Conditions that narrow the result set; combine clauses with the and / or logical operators. Reference fields by their response dotpath (e.g. metrics.organic.pos_1).
[⚠️Note:源码描述的运算符与最大条件数在各接口间不一致且部分符号被渲染截断，具体可用运算符与上限请以原生 description 为准，待研发确认。]

order_by.description: Sorting rules over the same fields usable in filters; each rule pairs a field with an asc or desc direction.

tag.description: Free-form label you attach to the task so you can match it to its result later; echoed back in the response data object.

post.description: Returns a comprehensive Google data profile for each supplied keyword, including cost-per-click, paid competition, search volume, search intent, monthly search history, plus SERP and backlink information; optional clickstream metrics are available via include_clickstream_data.

keyword.description: Seed keyword to expand from; specify it in lowercase using UTF-8 encoding.

location_name.description: Geographic target given by its full location name; supply either this or location_code. Look up valid names via the locations_and_languages endpoint.

location_code.description: Geographic target given by its numeric location code; supply either this or location_name. Look up valid codes via the locations_and_languages endpoint.

language_name.description: Target language given by its full name; supply either this or language_code. Look up valid names via the locations_and_languages endpoint.

language_code.description: Target language given by its short code; supply either this or language_name. Look up valid codes via the locations_and_languages endpoint.

include_seed_keyword.description: When true, also returns data for the seed keyword itself in the seed_keyword_data block of the response.

include_serp_info.description: When true, attaches a serp_info block per keyword with SERP-level data such as result count, a check URL, and the SERP feature types present.

include_clickstream_data.description: When true, enriches the response with clickstream-based metrics (such as clickstream search volume and gender/age distributions). Leaving it off keeps those fields empty.

exact_match.description: When true, returned keywords must contain the exact seed phrase (other words may appear before or after it).

ignore_synonyms.description: When true, collapses highly similar keywords and returns only the core keyword of each cluster.

filters.description:
Conditions that narrow the result set; combine clauses with the and / or logical operators. Reference fields by their response dotpath (e.g. metrics.organic.pos_1).
[⚠️Note:源码描述的运算符与最大条件数在各接口间不一致且部分符号被渲染截断，具体可用运算符与上限请以原生 description 为准，待研发确认。]

order_by.description: Sorting rules over the same fields usable in filters; each rule pairs a field with an asc or desc direction.

limit.description: Caps how many records are returned in the results array.

offset.description: Skips this many leading records in the results array, for paging through results.

offset_token.description: Pagination cursor for fetching subsequent pages beyond the offset limit; pass back the offset_token value returned in the previous response. Each token is single-use for the next page.

tag.description: Free-form label you attach to the task so you can match it to its result later; echoed back in the response data object.

target.description: Target domain or subdomain to analyze; specify it without the https:// scheme and without the www. prefix.

location_name.description: Geographic target given by its full location name; supply either this or location_code. Look up valid names via the locations_and_languages endpoint.

location_code.description: Geographic target given by its numeric location code; supply either this or location_name. Look up valid codes via the locations_and_languages endpoint.

language_name.description: Target language given by its full name; supply either this or language_code. Look up valid names via the locations_and_languages endpoint.

language_code.description: Target language given by its short code; supply either this or language_name. Look up valid codes via the locations_and_languages endpoint.

include_serp_info.description: When true, attaches a serp_info block per keyword with SERP-level data such as result count, a check URL, and the SERP feature types present.

include_subdomains.description: When true, keywords from the domain's subdomains are included; when false, only the exact host is considered.

include_clickstream_data.description: When true, enriches the response with clickstream-based metrics (such as clickstream search volume and gender/age distributions). Leaving it off keeps those fields empty.

limit.description: Caps how many records are returned in the results array.

offset.description: Skips this many leading records in the results array, for paging through results.

offset_token.description: Pagination cursor for fetching subsequent pages beyond the offset limit; pass back the offset_token value returned in the previous response. Each token is single-use for the next page.

filters.description:
Conditions that narrow the result set; combine clauses with the and / or logical operators. Reference fields by their response dotpath (e.g. metrics.organic.pos_1).
[⚠️Note:源码描述的运算符与最大条件数在各接口间不一致且部分符号被渲染截断，具体可用运算符与上限请以原生 description 为准，待研发确认。]

order_by.description: Sorting rules over the same fields usable in filters; each rule pairs a field with an asc or desc direction.

tag.description: Free-form label you attach to the task so you can match it to its result later; echoed back in the response data object.

post.description: Returns the keywords surfaced in Google's “searches related to” block for a seed keyword, expanded recursively by search depth, each enriched with keyword metrics, to grow a keyword set around a seed term.

keyword.description: Seed keyword to expand from; specify it in lowercase using UTF-8 encoding.

location_name.description: Geographic target given by its full location name; supply either this or location_code. Look up valid names via the locations_and_languages endpoint.

depth.description: Recursion depth for related-keyword expansion: higher levels traverse further into related searches and return progressively more keywords.

include_seed_keyword.description: When true, also returns data for the seed keyword itself in the seed_keyword_data block of the response.

include_serp_info.description: When true, attaches a serp_info block per keyword with SERP-level data such as result count, a check URL, and the SERP feature types present.

include_clickstream_data.description: When true, enriches the response with clickstream-based metrics (such as clickstream search volume and gender/age distributions). Leaving it off keeps those fields empty.

ignore_synonyms.description: When true, collapses highly similar keywords and returns only the core keyword of each cluster.

replace_with_core_keyword.description: When true, serp_info and related_keywords are returned for the core keyword of the cluster the seed belongs to; when false, for the seed keyword itself.

filters.description:
Conditions that narrow the result set; combine clauses with the and / or logical operators. Reference fields by their response dotpath (e.g. metrics.organic.pos_1).

target.description: Target domain or subdomain to analyze; specify it without the https:// scheme and without the www. prefix.

limit.description: Caps how many records are returned in the results array.

offset.description: Skips this many leading records in the results array, for paging through results.

historical_serp_mode.description: Controls which SERPs are counted: live keeps only SERPs where the target currently ranks, while lost keeps SERPs where it ranked before but no longer does.

ignore_synonyms.description: When true, collapses highly similar keywords and returns only the core keyword of each cluster.

location_name.description: Geographic target given by its full location name; supply either this or location_code. Look up valid names via the locations_and_languages endpoint.

location_code.description: Geographic target given by its numeric location code; supply either this or location_name. Look up valid codes via the locations_and_languages endpoint.

language_name.description: Target language given by its full name; supply either this or language_code. Look up valid names via the locations_and_languages endpoint.

language_code.description: Target language given by its short code; supply either this or language_name. Look up valid codes via the locations_and_languages endpoint.

include_serp_info.description: When true, attaches a serp_info block per keyword with SERP-level data such as result count, a check URL, and the SERP feature types present.

include_clickstream_data.description: When true, enriches the response with clickstream-based metrics (such as clickstream search volume and gender/age distributions). Leaving it off keeps those fields empty.

ignore_synonyms.description: When true, collapses highly similar keywords and returns only the core keyword of each cluster.

filters.description:
Conditions that narrow the result set; combine clauses with the and / or logical operators. Reference fields by their response dotpath (e.g. metrics.organic.pos_1).
[⚠️Note:源码描述的运算符与最大条件数在各接口间不一致且部分符号被渲染截断，具体可用运算符与上限请以原生 description 为准，待研发确认。]

order_by.description: Sorting rules over the same fields usable in filters; each rule pairs a field with an asc or desc direction.

tag.description: Free-form label you attach to the task so you can match it to its result later; echoed back in the response data object.

limit.description: Caps how many records are returned in the results array.

offset.description: Skips this many leading records in the results array, for paging through results.

offset_token.description: Pagination cursor for fetching subsequent pages beyond the offset limit; pass back the offset_token value returned in the previous response. Each token is single-use for the next page.

tasks.result.items.referring_links_types.description: Breakdown of referring links by link type (e.g. anchor, image, redirect).

tasks.result.items.referring_links_attri.description: Breakdown of referring links by their HTML attributes (e.g. nofollow, sponsored).

tasks.result.items.referring_links_platf.description: Breakdown of referring links by the platform type of their source site.

tasks.result.items.referring_links_seman.description: Breakdown of referring links by the semantic location on the source page (e.g. content, footer).

tasks.result.items.referring_links_count.description: Breakdown of referring links by the country of their source.

tasks.result.items.referring_pages_nofol.description: Number of referring pages whose links are all nofollow.

target.description: Domain, subdomain or webpage whose backlink data you want. Specify a domain/subdomain without the `http(s)://` scheme and without `www.`; specify a page as an absolute URL including the scheme.

limit.description: Page size: how many items to return in this call. Combine with `offset` to page through the full result set.

offset.description: Number of leading items to skip before returning results, for offset-based pagination together with `limit`.

internal_list_limit.description: Caps how many elements are returned inside the nested aggregation arrays of each item (e.g. `referring_links_tld`, `referring_links_types`); lower it to keep responses small.

backlinks_status_type.description: Selects which backlinks feed both the returned list and the aggregated metrics: `all` counts every known link, `live` only links confirmed in the latest check, `lost` only links that have disappeared.

filters.description: Conditions applied to the returned items, combined with `and`/`or` logical operators (up to 8). Reference any item field by name to narrow large result sets.

order_by.description: Sorting rules for the returned items; each rule pairs a field with `asc` or `desc`, up to three rules applied in order.

backlinks_filters.description: Pre-filters the raw backlinks that go into the aggregated metrics for the target, so metrics can be computed over a custom subset (e.g. dofollow-only).

include_subdomains.description: When true, subdomains of the target are included in the search; set false to restrict analysis to the target host only.

include_indirect_links.description: When true, results also count indirect links — links to a page that redirects to the target or points to the target's canonical page.

exclude_internal_backlinks.description: When true, links coming from the target's own subdomains are dropped so the same domain is not reported as its own referrer; set false to keep such internal links.

rank_scale.description: Chooses the numeric scale for all rank-style metrics (`rank`, `domain_from_rank`, `page_from_rank`): `one_hundred` for a 0–100 scale or `one_thousand` for a 0–1000 scale.

tag.description: Free-text label you attach to the task to correlate it with its result; echoed back in the response `data` object.

post.description: Returns the individual backlinks pointing to a target with full per-link detail (source page, anchor, attributes, ranks, status). The core endpoint for inspecting a target's link profile link by link.

target.description: Domain, subdomain or webpage whose backlink data you want. Specify a domain/subdomain without the `http(s)://` scheme and without `www.`; specify a page as an absolute URL including the scheme.

mode.description: Grouping of the returned backlinks: `as_is` returns every backlink, `one_per_domain` keeps one per referring domain, `one_per_anchor` keeps one per anchor text.

custom_mode.description: Fine-grained grouping that returns a fixed number of backlinks per chosen field; when set it overrides `mode`.

filters.description: Conditions applied to the returned items, combined with `and`/`or` logical operators (up to 8). Reference any item field by name to narrow large result sets.

order_by.description: Sorting rules for the returned items; each rule pairs a field with `asc` or `desc`, up to three rules applied in order.

offset.description: Number of leading items to skip before returning results, for offset-based pagination together with `limit`.

search_after_token.description: Continuation token for deep pagination beyond the 20,000-result offset cap. Pass the token from the previous response, keeping all other parameters identical, to fetch the next page.

limit.description: Page size: how many items to return in this call. Combine with `offset` to page through the full result set.

backlinks_status_type.description: Selects which backlinks feed both the returned list and the aggregated metrics: `all` counts every known link, `live` only links confirmed in the latest check, `lost` only links that have disappeared.

include_subdomains.description: When true, subdomains of the target are included in the search; set false to restrict analysis to the target host only.

exclude_internal_backlinks.description: When true, links coming from the target's own subdomains are dropped so the same domain is not reported as its own referrer; set false to keep such internal links.

rank_scale.description: Chooses the numeric scale for all rank-style metrics (`rank`, `domain_from_rank`, `page_from_rank`): `one_hundred` for a 0–100 scale or `one_thousand` for a 0–1000 scale.

tag.description: Free-text label you attach to the task to correlate it with its result; echoed back in the response `data` object.

post.description: Returns the total backlink count for up to 1000 targets in a single request — a fast way to compare link volume across many domains or pages.

targets.description: Set of domains, subdomains or webpages to query in one bulk request, each formatted the same way as `target`.

exclude_targets.description: Targets to exclude: results keep only referrers that link to `targets` but not to any entry here, useful for finding link gaps unique to your set.

filters.description: Conditions applied to the returned items, combined with `and`/`or` logical operators (up to 8). Reference any item field by name to narrow large result sets.

order_by.description: Sorting rules for the returned items; each rule pairs a field with `asc` or `desc`, up to three rules applied in order.

offset.description: Number of leading items to skip before returning results, for offset-based pagination together with `limit`.

limit.description: Page size: how many items to return in this call. Combine with `offset` to page through the full result set.

internal_list_limit.description: Caps how many elements are returned inside the nested aggregation arrays of each item (e.g. `referring_links_tld`, `referring_links_types`); lower it to keep responses small.

backlinks_status_type.description: Selects which backlinks feed both the returned list and the aggregated metrics: `all` counts every known link, `live` only links confirmed in the latest check, `lost` only links that have disappeared.

backlinks_filters.description: Pre-filters the raw backlinks that go into the aggregated metrics for the target, so metrics can be computed over a custom subset (e.g. dofollow-only).

include_subdomains.description: When true, subdomains of the target are included in the search; set false to restrict analysis to the target host only.

include_indirect_links.description: When true, results also count indirect links — links to a page that redirects to the target or points to the target's canonical page.

exclude_internal_backlinks.description: When true, links coming from the target's own subdomains are dropped so the same domain is not reported as its own referrer; set false to keep such internal links.

intersection_mode.description: How results across the specified targets are combined: `all` bases results on all backlinks, `partial` only on backlinks shared across targets.

rank_scale.description: Chooses the numeric scale for all rank-style metrics (`rank`, `domain_from_rank`, `page_from_rank`): `one_hundred` for a 0–100 scale or `one_thousand` for a 0–1000 scale.

tag.description: Free-text label you attach to the task to correlate it with its result; echoed back in the response `data` object.

post.description: Returns the pages of a domain that have backlinks, each with page metadata and a per-page backlink summary — useful for finding the most-linked pages of a site.

internal_list_limit.description: Caps how many elements are returned inside the nested aggregation arrays of each item (e.g. `referring_links_tld`, `referring_links_types`); lower it to keep responses small.

backlinks_status_type.description: Selects which backlinks feed both the returned list and the aggregated metrics: `all` counts every known link, `live` only links confirmed in the latest check, `lost` only links that have disappeared.

filters.description: Conditions applied to the returned items, combined with `and`/`or` logical operators (up to 8). Reference any item field by name to narrow large result sets.

order_by.description: Sorting rules for the returned items; each rule pairs a field with `asc` or `desc`, up to three rules applied in order.

backlinks_filters.description: Pre-filters the raw backlinks that go into the aggregated metrics for the target, so metrics can be computed over a custom subset (e.g. dofollow-only).

include_subdomains.description: When true, subdomains of the target are included in the search; set false to restrict analysis to the target host only.

exclude_internal_backlinks.description: When true, links coming from the target's own subdomains are dropped so the same domain is not reported as its own referrer; set false to keep such internal links.

rank_scale.description: Chooses the numeric scale for all rank-style metrics (`rank`, `domain_from_rank`, `page_from_rank`): `one_hundred` for a 0–100 scale or `one_thousand` for a 0–1000 scale.

tag.description: Free-text label you attach to the task to correlate it with its result; echoed back in the response `data` object.

tasks.data.description: Echo of the request parameters used to create this task, for traceability.

tasks.result.description: Result payload for this task; the actual OnPage data is nested inside.

target.description: Domain of the website to crawl, without scheme or trailing slash (e.g. `example.com`).

max_crawl_pages.description: Upper bound on how many pages the crawler will fetch for this task; the crawl stops once the limit is reached.

start_url.description: First page the crawler visits; if omitted the crawl starts from the target home page.

force_sitewide_checks.description: Run site-wide checks even when only a subset of pages is crawled.

priority_urls.description: URLs the crawler should fetch first, ahead of normal queue order.

max_crawl_depth.description: Maximum link depth from the start page that the crawler will follow.

crawl_delay.description: Delay the crawler waits between consecutive page requests, to ease load on the target server.

store_raw_html.description: Whether to keep each page's raw HTML so it can later be retrieved via the raw_html endpoint.

enable_content_parsing.description: Whether to parse page content into structured blocks during the crawl, enabling the content_parsing endpoint.

support_cookies.description: Whether the crawler accepts and sends cookies while crawling.

accept_language.description: Value sent in the crawler's Accept-Language header, controlling the language variant of fetched pages.

custom_robots_txt.description: Custom robots.txt content the crawler should obey instead of the site's own.

robots_txt_merge_mode.description: How custom_robots_txt combines with the site's robots.txt: enum values select merge vs. override behavior.

custom_user_agent.description: User-Agent string the crawler presents to the target server.

browser_preset.description: Named device preset that sets viewport and rendering defaults; enum values map to common device profiles.

browser_screen_width.description: Viewport width in pixels used when rendering the page in a browser.

browser_screen_height.description: Viewport height in pixels used when rendering the page in a browser.

browser_screen_scale_factor.description: Device pixel ratio applied to the rendering viewport.

respect_sitemap.description: Whether the crawler uses the site's sitemap to discover pages.

custom_sitemap.description: URL of a sitemap to crawl instead of auto-discovering the site's own.

crawl_sitemap_only.description: Restrict the crawl to URLs listed in the sitemap, skipping link discovery.

load_resources.description: Whether the crawler also fetches page resources (images, scripts, stylesheets) for analysis.

enable_www_redirect_check.description: Whether to test the www / non-www redirect configuration of the site.

enable_javascript.description: Whether to execute JavaScript when rendering pages, capturing client-rendered content.

enable_xhr.description: Whether to capture XHR/fetch requests made by the page during rendering.

enable_browser_rendering.description: Whether to render pages in a real browser instead of a plain HTTP fetch.

disable_cookie_popup.description: Whether to suppress cookie-consent popups before capturing the page.

custom_js.description: Custom JavaScript executed on each page after load; its output is returned in custom_js_response.

validate_micromarkup.description: Whether to validate structured-data markup on crawled pages.

allow_subdomains.description: Whether the crawler may follow links into subdomains of the target.

allowed_subdomains.description: Explicit list of subdomains the crawler is permitted to enter.

disallowed_subdomains.description: List of subdomains the crawler must not enter.

check_spell.description: Whether to run spell-checking on page text content.

check_spell_language.description: Language used for spell-checking, by language code.

check_spell_exceptions.description: Words to exclude from spell-checking (e.g. brand names).

calculate_keyword_density.description: Whether to compute keyword-density statistics during the crawl.

checks_threshold.description: Custom thresholds overriding the defaults used to flag on-page checks.

disable_sitewide_checks.description: Site-wide check names to skip for this task.

disable_page_checks.description: Per-page check names to skip for this task.

switch_pool.description: Whether to retry from a different IP pool if the target blocks the initial request.

return_despite_timeout.description: Whether to return partial results when a page exceeds the load timeout instead of failing it.

tag.description: Free-text label you attach to the task to identify it in later listings and callbacks.

pingback_url.description: URL the API calls when the task completes, so you can collect results without polling.

tasks.result.items.reason.description: Explanation of why the page is non-indexable or why the resource is uncrawlable.

tasks.result.items.status_code.description: HTTP status code returned when the page was fetched.

tasks.result.items.fetch_time.description: Timestamp when the page or resource was fetched.

tasks.result.items.meta.description: Metadata of the resource (e.g. image dimensions, content type, title).

tasks.result.items.meta.content_type.description: MIME content type the resource was served with.

tasks.result.items.meta.expected_content.description: Content types expected for the resource based on context.

id.description: Task id returned by task_post; identifies which crawl's data to read or act on.

limit.description: Maximum number of items to return in this response.

offset.description: Number of leading items to skip, for paging through results.

order_by.description: Sorting rules applied to the returned items, as field/direction pairs.

filters.description: Array of conditions used to narrow the returned items; usable fields are listed by available_filters.

post.description: Return the per-resource load-timing waterfall for a single crawled page (by task id and url), exposing how each resource contributed to total load time.

version.description: API version string that produced this response; useful when reporting issues.

status_code.description: Overall response status code; check it before reading the payload and handle error cases.

status_message.description: Human-readable message accompanying the overall status code.

time.description: Server-side processing time for the request.

cost.description: Total billed cost of this response across all tasks, in USD.

tasks_count.description: Number of task entries contained in the tasks array.

tasks_error.description: How many tasks finished with an error; if non-zero, inspect each task's own status fields.

tasks.description: List of task results; one entry per task created by the request.

tasks.id.description: Unique identifier (UUID) of this task; reuse it to fetch the task's data from other OnPage endpoints.

tasks.status_code.description: Per-task status code; check it to tell whether this individual task succeeded.

tasks.status_message.description: Human-readable message for this task's status code.

tasks.time.description: Server-side processing time spent on this task.

tasks.cost.description: Billed cost attributed to this task, in USD.

tasks.result_count.description: Number of entries in this task's result array.

tasks.path.description: Endpoint path segments that were called to create this task; echoes the request routing.

tasks.data.description: Echo of the request parameters used to create this task, for traceability.

tasks.result.description: Result payload for this task; the actual OnPage data is nested inside.

tasks.result.crawl_progress.description: Crawl lifecycle state of the task; tells whether the crawl is in progress or finished.

tasks.result.crawl_status.description: Crawl progress snapshot: page-count limit, pages crawled, and pages still queued.

tasks.result.crawl_status.max_crawl_page.description: Page-count limit configured for this crawl.

tasks.result.crawl_status.pages_in_queue.description: Number of pages still waiting to be crawled.

tasks.result.crawl_status.pages_crawled.description: Number of pages fetched so far.

tasks.result.items_count.description: Number of items returned in this result page.

tasks.result.items.description: Array of result records for this endpoint; structure depends on the endpoint called.

tasks.result.items.page_url.description: URL of the page the parsed content belongs to.

tasks.result.items.time_to_interactive.description: Time until the page became interactive (TTI), in milliseconds.

tasks.result.items.dom_complete.description: Time at which DOM construction completed, in milliseconds.

tasks.result.items.connection_time.description: Time to establish the network connection, in milliseconds.

tasks.result.items.time_to_secure_connec.description: Time to complete the TLS handshake, in milliseconds.

tasks.result.items.request_sent_time.description: Time at which the request was sent, in milliseconds.

tasks.result.items.waiting_time.description: Time spent waiting for the server's first byte (TTFB), in milliseconds.

tasks.result.items.download_time.description: Time spent downloading the response body, in milliseconds.

tasks.result.items.duration_time.description: Total time the request took end to end, in milliseconds.

tasks.result.items.fetch_start.description: Offset at which fetching started, in milliseconds.

tasks.result.items.fetch_end.description: Offset at which fetching finished, in milliseconds.

tasks.result.items.resources.description: Per-resource records loaded by the page, with timing and metadata.

tasks.result.items.resources.resource_ty.description: Type of the resource (e.g. image, script, stylesheet, font).

tasks.result.items.resources.url.description: URL of this page or record.

tasks.result.items.resources.initiator.description: URL or component that triggered loading of this resource.

tasks.result.items.resources.duration_ti.description: Total time the request took end to end, in milliseconds.

tasks.result.items.resources.fetch_start.description: Offset at which fetching started, in milliseconds.

tasks.result.items.resources.fetch_end.description: Offset at which fetching finished, in milliseconds.

tasks.result.items.resources.location.description: Position where the resource is referenced in the source, or the resource location object.

tasks.result.items.resources.location.li.description: Line number in the source where the reference or error occurs.

tasks.result.items.resources.location.of.description: Horizontal pixel offset of the element.
Vertical pixel offset of the element.

tasks.result.items.resources.is_render_b.description: Whether the resource blocks rendering until it finishes loading.

url.description: Page URL to act on; for read endpoints it selects a single crawled page, for live endpoints it is the page to fetch now.

tag.description: Free-text label you attach to the task to identify it in later listings and callbacks.

tasks.result.languages.description: Distribution of citations by language with the citation count per language; resolve language codes via the Languages endpoint.

page_type.description: Restrict results to these page types. Possible values: "ecommerce", "news", "blogs", "message-boards", "organization".

search_mode.description: How citations are grouped: `as_is` returns every citation, `one_per_domain` returns one citation per domain.

internal_list_limit.description: Single cap applied to internal arrays (e.g. countries, languages, content_languages, keywords); when set, it overrides the per-level internal limits.

date_to.description: End date of the time range (format yyyy-mm-dd); defaults to today when omitted. Example: "2019-01-15".

date_group.description: Granularity for grouping the time series. Possible values: day, week, month (default month).

initial_dataset_filters.description: Filters applied to the underlying Search dataset before trend/summary aggregation (up to 8), combined with and/or logical operators.

rank_scale.description: Scale used to present rank values; choose whether ranks are reported on a 0-100 or 0-1000 scale.

tasks.description: Array holding the individual task results of this call; the Live endpoints submit one task per call, so expect a single entry.

tasks.id.description: UUID identifying this task within DataForSEO's system; use it to correlate the result with your request.

tasks.status_code.description: Per-task status code (range 10000-60000). `20000` means the task completed successfully; other values indicate this specific task failed.

tasks.status_message.description: Human-readable message for this task's status; consult it when the task code is not a success value.

tasks.time.description: Execution time of this individual task, in seconds.

tasks.cost.description: Amount charged for this individual task, in USD.

tasks.result_count.description: Number of elements in this task's `tasks.result` array.

tasks.path.description: API path segments that were called, echoing how the request was routed.

tasks.data.description: Echo of the parameters you submitted in the POST request body, useful for correlating the response with its input.

tasks.result.description: Array carrying this task's result records; the fields below describe one record.

tasks.result.offset_token.description: Token to fetch the next page of citations; pass it as the request `offset_token` to page past the deep-offset limit and retrieve over 10,000 results across requests.

tasks.result.total_count.description: Total number of records in the database matching your request, which may exceed the number actually returned.

tasks.result.items_count.description: Number of records returned in the `items` array of this response.

tasks.result.items.description: Array of individual citations and their associated content and ranking data.

tasks.result.items.type.description: Element type marker for the citation record; fixed value `content_analysis_search`.

tasks.result.items.url.description: URL of the page where the citation was found.

tasks.result.items.main_domain.description: Main (root) domain of the citing page.

tasks.result.items.url_rank.description: Backlink-based rank of the specific URL from DataForSEO's Backlink Index; a higher value reflects a stronger backlink profile for the page.

tasks.result.items.spam_score.description: Backlink spam score of the URL from DataForSEO's Backlink Index; a higher value indicates a riskier backlink profile.

tasks.result.items.domain_rank.description: Backlink-based rank of the citing domain from DataForSEO's Backlink Index.

tasks.result.items.fetch_time.description: Time the DataForSEO crawler visited the page, in UTC `yyyy-mm-dd hh-mm-ss +00:00` format. Example: 2017-01-24 13:20:59 +00:00.

tasks.result.items.country.description: Country code of the domain's registration; resolve via the Locations endpoint.

tasks.result.items.language.description: Main language of the domain; resolve via the Languages endpoint.

tasks.result.items.score.description: Citation prominence score derived from url_rank, domain_rank and keyword presence in title, main_title, url and snippet; a higher score means a more valuable citation.

tasks.result.items.page_category.description: Product and service categories relevant to the citing page; resolve codes via the Categories endpoint.

tasks.result.items.page_types.description: Page types assigned to the citing page (e.g. ecommerce, news, blogs, message-boards, organization).

tasks.result.items.ratings.description: Ratings extracted from the page's microdata markup.

tasks.result.items.social_metrics.description: Social media engagement metrics for the page, derived from platform-supported embeds.

tasks.result.items.content_info.description: Detailed information about the specific citation found at the URL.

tasks.result.items.content_info.content_.description: Type of content the citation appears in. Example: page_content, comment.

tasks.result.items.content_info.title.description: Title of the content block carrying the citation.

tasks.result.items.content_info.previous.description: Title of the content block preceding the citation, useful for locating it in the page structure.

tasks.result.items.content_info.level.description: Heading level of the title, from 1 (top) to 6 (bottom), matching the page's h-tag depth.

tasks.result.items.content_info.author.description: Author credited for the content, when available.

tasks.result.items.content_info.snippet.description: Excerpt of the content surrounding the citation.

tasks.result.items.content_info.social_m.description: Social media engagement metrics for this content block, derived from platform-supported embeds.

tasks.result.items.content_info.highligh.description: Portion of the snippet that matches the target keyword, highlighted for emphasis.

tasks.result.items.content_info.language.description: Language of the content; resolve via the Languages endpoint.

tasks.result.items.content_info.sentimen.description: Emotional reactions (e.g. anger, happiness, love, sadness) detected for this citation, each with its probability index.

tasks.result.items.content_info.connotat.description: Sentiment polarity (positive, negative, neutral) detected for this citation, each with its probability index.

tasks.result.items.content_info.text_cat.description: Text categories assigned to this content; resolve codes via the Categories endpoint.

tasks.result.items.content_info.date_pub.description: Time the content was published, in UTC `yyyy-mm-dd hh-mm-ss +00:00` format. Example: 2017-01-24 13:20:59 +00:00.

tasks.result.items.content_info.content_.description: Content quality score derived from the number of words, sentences and characters in the content.

tasks.result.items.content_info.semantic.description: Semantic HTML element where the keyword citation sits. Example: article, header.

tasks.result.items.content_info.rating.description: Rating associated with this content block.

tasks.result.items.content_info.rating.n.description: Rating scale name. Possible values: Max5, Percents, CustomMax.

tasks.result.items.content_info.rating.r.description: The rating value on its scale.

tasks.result.items.content_info.rating.m.description: Maximum possible value for the named rating scale.

tasks.result.items.content_info.rating.r.description: Number of votes behind the rating.
Rating normalized relative to its maximum, for cross-scale comparison.

tasks.result.items.content_info.group_da.description: Publication date or first-crawl time used to group citations chronologically.

status_code.description: Overall response status code for the request. Use it to detect success vs. error conditions; see the DataForSEO status-code reference for the full list.

tasks.time.description: Processing time of this individual task, in seconds.

tasks.cost.description: Cost of this individual task, in USD.

tasks.result_count.description: Number of elements in this task's `result` array.

tasks.result.description: Array of result objects for the task. For a just-posted task this is null and is populated once the task completes.

asin.description: Amazon Standard Identification Number (ASIN) of the product to look up. Obtain it from the Amazon Products endpoint; ASINs are assigned dynamically by Amazon (e.g. `B085RFFC9Q`).

priority.description: Execution priority of the task: `1` normal (default), `2` high. High priority speeds up processing but incurs additional charges.

location_name.description: Full location name for the search engine (e.g. `London,England,United Kingdom`). Use this when not setting `location_code`/`location_coordinate`; fetch valid values from the corresponding locations endpoint.

location_code.description: Numeric location code for the search engine (e.g. `9045969`). Use this when not setting `location_name`/`location_coordinate`; fetch valid values from the corresponding locations endpoint.

location_coordinate.description: GPS coordinates as `latitude,longitude,radius` (e.g. `53.476225,-2.243572,200`). Use instead of `location_name`/`location_code`; latitude/longitude allow up to 7 decimals and radius must be at least 199.9.

language_name.description: Full language name for the search engine (e.g. `English (United Kingdom)`). Use when not setting `language_code`; fetch valid values from the corresponding languages endpoint.

language_code.description: Language code for the search engine (e.g. `en_GB` for Amazon, `en` for app stores). Use when not setting `language_name`; fetch valid values from the corresponding languages endpoint.

se_domain.description: Custom search-engine domain (e.g. `amazon.co.uk`, `google.de`). Optional — by default the relevant domain is chosen automatically from the location and language.

tag.description: User-defined task label (up to 255 chars) echoed back in the response `data` object, so you can match results to the task you posted.

postback_url.description: Callback URL to which a gzip-compressed POST with the task results is sent once the task completes. Supports `$id` and url-encoded `$tag` placeholders that are substituted before sending.

postback_data.description: Result datatype delivered to `postback_url`; required when `postback_url` is set. Selects which task-result format is pushed (e.g. `advanced`, `html`).

pingback_url.description: Notification URL pinged via GET when the task completes. Supports `$id` and url-encoded `$tag` placeholders substituted before the request is sent.

keyword.description: Search query (product name or app keyword) to run. Up to 700 characters; `%##` sequences are URL-decoded and `+` becomes a space, so encode a literal `%` as `%25` and a literal `+` as `%2B`.

url.description: Direct search-query URL to parse instead of structured parameters. The URL must already encode the exact language and location; this method is the hardest to process and generally not recommended.

priority.description: Execution priority of the task: `1` normal (default), `2` high. High priority speeds up processing but incurs additional charges.

location_name.description: Full location name for the search engine (e.g. `London,England,United Kingdom`). Use this when not setting `location_code`/`location_coordinate`; fetch valid values from the corresponding locations endpoint.

location_coordinate.description: GPS coordinates as `latitude,longitude,radius` (e.g. `53.476225,-2.243572,200`). Use instead of `location_name`/`location_code`; latitude/longitude allow up to 7 decimals and radius must be at least 199.9.

language_name.description: Full language name for the search engine (e.g. `English (United Kingdom)`). Use when not setting `language_code`; fetch valid values from the corresponding languages endpoint.

language_code.description: Language code for the search engine (e.g. `en_GB` for Amazon, `en` for app stores). Use when not setting `language_name`; fetch valid values from the corresponding languages endpoint.

se_domain.description: Custom search-engine domain (e.g. `amazon.co.uk`, `google.de`). Optional — by default the relevant domain is chosen automatically from the location and language.

depth.description: Parsing depth — how many results/reviews/apps to return. Best set in multiples of the per-page batch size; raising it above one batch may add charges if extra results are returned. Limits vary by endpoint.

max_crawl_pages.description: Maximum number of SERP pages to crawl (up to 7). Works together with `depth` to bound how much of the results page set is retrieved.

department.description: Amazon department to scope product listings to (e.g. `Electronics`, `Books`, `Home & Kitchen`).

search_param.description: Extra raw search-URL parameters for fine-grained control (e.g. price bounds, sort order). Ignored if `price_min`, `price_max`, or `sort_by` are also provided.

price_min.description: Minimum product price filter for the query (e.g. `5`). When set, `search_param` is ignored.

price_max.description: Maximum product price filter for the query (e.g. `100`). When set, `search_param` is ignored.

sort_by.description: Sorting rule: `relevance`, `price_low_to_high`, `price_high_to_low`, `featured`, `avg_customer_review`, or `newest_arrival`. When set, `search_param` is ignored.

tag.description: User-defined task label (up to 255 chars) echoed back in the response `data` object, so you can match results to the task you posted.

postback_url.description: Callback URL to which a gzip-compressed POST with the task results is sent once the task completes. Supports `$id` and url-encoded `$tag` placeholders that are substituted before sending.

postback_data.description: Result datatype delivered to `postback_url`; required when `postback_url` is set. Selects which task-result format is pushed (e.g. `advanced`, `html`).

pingback_url.description: Notification URL pinged via GET when the task completes. Supports `$id` and url-encoded `$tag` placeholders substituted before the request is sent.

data_docid.description: Unique identifier of the Google Shopping SERP data element (e.g. `13071766526042404278`); required if neither `product_id` nor `gid` is given. Best paired with `product_id` and `gid`; obtain it from the Google Shopping Products endpoint.

location_coordinate.description: GPS coordinates as `latitude,longitude,radius` (e.g. `53.476225,-2.243572,200`). Use instead of `location_name`/`location_code`; latitude/longitude allow up to 7 decimals and radius must be at least 199.9.

language_name.description: Full language name for the search engine (e.g. `English (United Kingdom)`). Use when not setting `language_code`; fetch valid values from the corresponding languages endpoint.

se_domain.description: Custom search-engine domain (e.g. `amazon.co.uk`, `google.de`). Optional — by default the relevant domain is chosen automatically from the location and language.

depth.description: Parsing depth — how many results/reviews/apps to return. Best set in multiples of the per-page batch size; raising it above one batch may add charges if extra results are returned. Limits vary by endpoint.

tasks.result.offset_token.description: Pagination token to pass into a subsequent POST request to retrieve large result sets (over 100,000) without timeouts.

tasks.result.items.item.description: Detailed information object for the app in this item.

tasks.result.items.item.rank_group.description: Rank within the group of items sharing the same `type`; items of differing types are skipped in this count.

tasks.result.items.item.rank_absolute.description: Absolute rank of the app across the entire returned list, regardless of type.

tasks.result.items.item.rating.description: Average rating object of the app.

tasks.result.items.item.rating.rating_ma.description: Maximum possible value for the rating scale (e.g. 5 for `Max5`).

tasks.result.items.item.price.current.description: Current price shown for the app.

tasks.result.items.item.price.max_value.description: Maximum price when the app is offered as a price range.

tasks.result.items.item.price.currency.description: ISO currency code of the listed price.

tasks.result.items.item.price.is_price_r.description: Indicates whether the price is given as a range rather than a single value.

tasks.result.items.item.is_free.description: Indicates whether the app is free.

tasks.result.items.item.main_category.description: Primary store category most relevant to the app.

tasks.result.items.item.categories.description: Additional store categories relevant to the app.

tasks.result.items.item.advisories.description: App advisory ratings and usage restrictions (e.g. content warnings).

tasks.result.items.item.developer_url.description: URL to the developer's page on the store.

tasks.result.items.item.minimum_os_versi.description: Minimum OS version required to install the app.

tasks.result.items.item.released_date.description: Date and time the app was first released, in UTC (`yyyy-mm-dd hh-mm-ss +00:00`).

tasks.result.items.item.last_update_date.description: Date and time the app was last updated, in UTC (`yyyy-mm-dd hh-mm-ss +00:00`).

tasks.result.items.item.update_notes.description: Latest update notes provided by the developer.

tasks.result.items.item.images.description: URLs to the screenshots/images shown on the app's store page.

tasks.result.items.item.similar_apps.description: List of apps similar to this app.

tasks.result.items.item.similar_apps.url.description: URL to the similar app's store page.

tasks.result.items.item.more_apps_by_dev.description: Other apps built by the same developer.
Identifier of the developer's other app.
Title of the developer's other app.
URL to the developer's other app store page.

categories.description: App-listing categories used to scope the search; up to 10 may be specified. Fetch valid values from the app-listings categories endpoint.

description.description: Keyword that must appear in the app description (up to 200 chars).

title.description: Keyword that must appear in the app title (up to 200 chars).

filters.description: Array of result-filtering conditions (up to 8), joined by `and`/`or`. Each condition is `[field, operator, value]` (e.g. `["rating.value",">",3]`); operators include comparison, `in`/`not_in`, `like`/`not_like`, `regex`. Fetch supported fields from the available-filters endpoint.

order_by.description: Result sorting rules using the same fields as `filters`, formatted as `field,asc|desc` (e.g. `["item.rating.value,desc"]`). Up to three rules per request.

limit.description: Maximum number of apps to return.

offset.description: Number of leading results to skip in the returned array (e.g. `10` omits the first ten). Recommended only up to ~10,000 results; beyond that use `offset_token`.

offset_token.description: Pagination token returned in the response of the previous request; pass it back to fetch subsequent results and avoid timeouts on very large result sets. When used, all other parameters must match the previous request, and each token is single-use.

description.description: Keyword that must appear in the app description (up to 200 chars).

title.description: Keyword that must appear in the app title (up to 200 chars).

filters.description: Array of result-filtering conditions (up to 8), joined by `and`/`or`. Each condition is `[field, operator, value]` (e.g. `["rating.value",">",3]`); operators include comparison, `in`/`not_in`, `like`/`not_like`, `regex`. Fetch supported fields from the available-filters endpoint.

order_by.description: Result sorting rules using the same fields as `filters`, formatted as `field,asc|desc` (e.g. `["item.rating.value,desc"]`). Up to three rules per request.

limit.description: Maximum number of apps to return.

offset.description: Number of leading results to skip in the returned array (e.g. `10` omits the first ten). Recommended only up to ~10,000 results; beyond that use `offset_token`.

offset_token.description: Pagination token returned in the response of the previous request; pass it back to fetch subsequent results and avoid timeouts on very large result sets. When used, all other parameters must match the previous request, and each token is single-use.

tag.description: User-defined task label (up to 255 chars) echoed back in the response `data` object, so you can match results to the task you posted.

id.description: Task identifier (UUID) returned when posting a task; use it with the matching Task GET endpoint to collect the results within the task retention window.

tasks.time.description: Processing time of this individual task, in seconds.

tasks.cost.description: Cost of this individual task, in USD.

tasks.result_count.description: Number of elements in this task's `result` array.

tasks.path.description: API URL path segments that identify the endpoint this task was routed to.

tasks.data.description: Echo of the parameters supplied when the task was created (POST body or GET URL parameters).

tasks.result.description: Array of result objects for the task. For a just-posted task this is null and is populated once the task completes.

app_id.description: Identifier of the target app on the store. For Google Play it is the package id from the app URL (e.g. `org.telegram.messenger`); for App Store it is the numeric id from the app URL (e.g. `835599320`).

location_name.description: Full location name for the search engine (e.g. `London,England,United Kingdom`). Use this when not setting `location_code`/`location_coordinate`; fetch valid values from the corresponding locations endpoint.

location_code.description: Numeric location code for the search engine (e.g. `9045969`). Use this when not setting `location_name`/`location_coordinate`; fetch valid values from the corresponding locations endpoint.

language_name.description: Full language name for the search engine (e.g. `English (United Kingdom)`). Use when not setting `language_code`; fetch valid values from the corresponding languages endpoint.

language_code.description: Language code for the search engine (e.g. `en_GB` for Amazon, `en` for app stores). Use when not setting `language_name`; fetch valid values from the corresponding languages endpoint.

priority.description: Execution priority of the task: `1` normal (default), `2` high. High priority speeds up processing but incurs additional charges.

depth.description: Parsing depth — how many results/reviews/apps to return. Best set in multiples of the per-page batch size; raising it above one batch may add charges if extra results are returned. Limits vary by endpoint.

rating.description: Filter reviews by star rating: `1`–`5` returns reviews with exactly that number of stars. By default all reviews are returned regardless of rating.

sort_by.description: Sorting of returned reviews: `most_recent` (most recent) or `most_helpful`. Default is `most_helpful`.

tag.description: User-defined task label (up to 255 chars) echoed back in the response `data` object, so you can match results to the task you posted.

postback_url.description: Callback URL to which a gzip-compressed POST with the task results is sent once the task completes. Supports `$id` and url-encoded `$tag` placeholders that are substituted before sending.

postback_data.description: Result datatype delivered to `postback_url`; required when `postback_url` is set. Selects which task-result format is pushed (e.g. `advanced`, `html`).

pingback_url.description: Notification URL pinged via GET when the task completes. Supports `$id` and url-encoded `$tag` placeholders substituted before the request is sent.
