post.description: Synchronously search DataForSEO's business-listings database by category, name, description, location and custom filters, returning matching local businesses with their profiles, ratings and contact details in a single request.

version.description: API version string that produced this response, e.g. `3.0.0`.

version.status_code.description: Overall status code of the response envelope (DataForSEO global code, distinct from the per-task `tasks.status_code`); `20000` indicates the request was accepted successfully.

version.status_message.description: Human-readable message accompanying the envelope `status_code`; read it when troubleshooting a non-success code.

version.time.description: Wall-clock processing time of the whole request, expressed in seconds.

version.cost.description: Total money charged for all tasks in this response, in US dollars.

version.tasks_count.description: Number of task objects contained in the `tasks` array.

version.tasks_error.description: Number of task objects in `tasks` that finished with an error; if non-zero, inspect each task's `status_code`.

tasks.description: List of task objects, one per posted task, each holding its own status and `result` payload.

tasks.id.description: Unique task identifier (UUID). Reuse it on the matching `task_get` endpoint to fetch this task's results within the retention window.

tasks.status_code.description: Per-task status code in the 10000-60000 range; `20000` means the task completed successfully while other values signal task-level errors.

tasks.status_message.description: Human-readable message explaining this task's `status_code`.

tasks.time.description: Processing time of this individual task, in seconds.

tasks.cost.description: Money charged for this individual task, in US dollars.

tasks.result_count.description: Number of elements in this task's `result` array.

tasks.path.description: URL path segments of the endpoint that produced this task, useful for tracing which call the task belongs to.

tasks.data.description: Echo of the parameters you supplied when posting the task, returned so you can correlate results with your input.

result.description: Array holding the actual data payload of the task.

result.total_count.description: Total number of matching results available in our database for your request.

result.count.description: Number of items in the `items` array.

result.offset.description: Offset position within the returned businesses array.

result.offset_token.description: Token enabling pagination: pass it on a follow-up task to retrieve the next batch of results; each token is single-use per follow-up.

items.description: Array of nested item elements belonging to this entry.

items.type.description: Type tag of this element, identifying its schema.

items.title.description: Title / name shown for the element in the SERP.

items.original_title.description: Original element title as shown by Google, before any translation.

items.description.description: Description of the business entity shown in the SERP.

items.category.description: Primary Google My Business category that best describes the entity's services.

items.category_ids.description: Global category IDs that stay constant regardless of the selected country.

items.additional_categories.description: Additional Google My Business categories detailing the entity's services.

items.cid.description: Google-defined client ID uniquely identifying the local establishment.

items.feature_id.description: Unique identifier of the element / establishment in the SERP.

items.address.description: Street address of the business entity.

items.address_info.description: Structured object breaking the address into individual components.

items.borough.description: Administrative district or borough the entity belongs to.

items.city.description: City where the entity is located.

items.zip.description: ZIP / postal code of the entity.

items.region.description: DMA region of the entity's location.

items.country_code.description: ISO country code of the entity's location.

items.place_id.description: Google Place ID of the local establishment featured in the element.

items.phone.description: Phone number of the business entity.

items.url.description: Absolute URL associated with this element.

items.logo.description: URL of the logo shown in the Google My Business profile.

items.main_image.description: URL of the main image shown in the Google My Business profile.

items.total_photos.description: Total number of images featured in the Google My Business profile.

items.snippet.description: Additional descriptive snippet about the business entity.

items.latitude.description: Latitude coordinate of the entity on Google Maps.

items.longitude.description: Longitude coordinate of the entity on Google Maps.

items.is_claimed.description: Whether the entity has been verified by its owner on Google Maps.

items.attributes.description: Service-detail checks for the entity, derived from user feedback and its `category`.

items.available_attributes.description: Attributes the entity is reported to offer.

items.unavailable_attributes.description: Attributes the entity is reported not to offer.

items.place_topics.description: Most-mentioned keywords from customer reviews mapped to how many reviews mention each.

items.rating.description: Rating object summarizing review-based popularity of the entity.

items.rating_type.description: Rating scale in use; values include `Max5`, `Percents`, `CustomMax`, indicating how `value` should be interpreted.

items.value.description: Numeric rating value, interpreted according to `rating_type`.

items.votes_count.description: Number of votes / reviews the rating is based on.

items.rating_max.description: Maximum possible value for the given `rating_type`.

items.hotel_rating.description: Hotel class rating ranging 1-5 stars; `null` when no class rating exists.

items.price_level.description: Relative price level; values: `inexpensive`, `moderate`, `expensive`, `very_expensive`; `null` when unknown.

items.rating_distribution.description: Rating-distribution placeholder; in hotel-search results this field is always `null` and exists only for interoperability with the Hotel Info endpoint.

items.people_also_search.description: Related business entities that users also searched for.

items.work_time.description: Object describing the entity's operating-hours details.

items.work_hours.description: Object describing the establishment's working hours.

popular_times.description: Object describing busy-hour patterns of the establishment.

popular_times.popular_times_by_days.description: Busy-hour breakdown for each day of the week.

popular_times.popular_index.description: Time-bound popularity index from 0 to 100; higher means busier.

local_business_links.description: List of available interactions with the business directly from the search results, such as booking, ordering or messaging links.

local_business_links.type.description: Type tag of this element, identifying its schema.

local_business_links.title.description: Title / name shown for the element in the SERP.

local_business_links.url.description: Absolute URL associated with this element.

contact_info.description: List of the business's available contacts (e.g. phone, website) you can use to reach it directly from search results.

contact_info.type.description: Type tag of this element, identifying its schema.

contact_info.value.description: Numeric rating value, interpreted according to `rating_type`.

contact_info.source.description: Data source of the contact entry.

contact_info.check_url.description: Direct URL to the search results, for verifying the data.

contact_info.last_updated_time.description: UTC timestamp when this contact data was last updated.

contact_info.first_seen.description: UTC timestamp when our crawler first encountered this element.

categories.description: Business categories used to filter the listings search; omit it to return all listings found in the location.

description.description: Business description to match (up to 200 characters); restricts results to listings whose description matches.

title.description: Business name to match (up to 200 characters); restricts results to listings whose name matches.

is_claimed.description: Filter by owner-verification status: `true` returns only businesses verified by their owner on Google Maps.

location_coordinate.description: GPS coordinates in `latitude,longitude` (optionally with a radius) setting the geographic context. Supply this or `location_name`/`location_code`.

filters.description: Array of filtering conditions (up to 8) combined with `and`/`or` logical operators to narrow the listings search.

order_by.description: Sorting rules for the results, using the same fields as `filters` with direction `asc` or `desc`.

limit.description: Maximum number of businesses to return. Default `100`, maximum `1000`.

offset.description: Number of leading businesses to skip in the results array. Default `0`.

offset_token.description: Pagination token returned in a prior response; pass it to fetch the next batch and avoid timeouts on very large result sets.

tag.description: Your own task label (up to 255 characters), echoed back in the response `data` so you can correlate results with requests.

result.location_code.description: Location code echoed from the POST request.

result.language_code.description: Language code echoed from the POST request.

result.check_url.description: Direct URL to the search engine results page, so you can verify the data against the live SERP.

result.datetime.description: UTC timestamp when the result was collected, formatted `yyyy-mm-dd hh-mm-ss +00:00`.

result.title.description: Name of the local establishment that the 'reviews' SERP element collects reviews for.

result.sub_title.description: Supplementary information (such as the address) shown beneath the title of the 'reviews' element.

result.rating.description: Aggregated rating of the entity, as shown in the SERP.

result.reviews_count.description: Total number of reviews available for the establishment.

result.items_count.description: Number of elements returned in the `items` array; raise the `depth` parameter at task creation to get more.

items.description: Review entries collected for the establishment; raise `depth` at task creation to retrieve more.

items.rank_group.description: Position of this entry within the group of elements sharing the same `type`.

items.xpath.description: XPath locating this review element within the parsed page.

items.review_text.description: Body text of the review (auto-translated when applicable).

items.domain.description: Domain of the source site where the review was posted.

post.summary: Setting Business Data Google Extended Reviews Tasks
post.description: Create an asynchronous task that collects an establishment's Google reviews, including reviews aggregated from third-party sites; poll the matching tasks_ready endpoint and then fetch results by task id.

version.description: API version string that produced this response, e.g. `3.0.0`.

version.status_code.description: Overall status code of the response envelope (DataForSEO global code, distinct from the per-task `tasks.status_code`); `20000` indicates the request was accepted successfully.

version.status_message.description: Human-readable message accompanying the envelope `status_code`; read it when troubleshooting a non-success code.

version.time.description: Wall-clock processing time of the whole request, expressed in seconds.

version.cost.description: Total money charged for all tasks in this response, in US dollars.

version.tasks_count.description: Number of task objects contained in the `tasks` array.

version.tasks_error.description: Number of task objects in `tasks` that finished with an error; if non-zero, inspect each task's `status_code`.

tasks.description: List of task objects, one per posted task, each holding its own status and `result` payload.

tasks.id.description: Unique task identifier (UUID). Reuse it on the matching `task_get` endpoint to fetch this task's results within the retention window.

tasks.status_code.description: Per-task status code in the 10000-60000 range; `20000` means the task completed successfully while other values signal task-level errors.

tasks.status_message.description: Human-readable message explaining this task's `status_code`.

tasks.time.description: Processing time of this individual task, in seconds.

tasks.cost.description: Money charged for this individual task, in US dollars.

tasks.result_count.description: Number of elements in this task's `result` array.

tasks.path.description: URL path segments of the endpoint that produced this task, useful for tracing which call the task belongs to.

tasks.data.description: Echo of the parameters you supplied when posting the task, returned so you can correlate results with your input.

result.description: Array holding the actual data payload of the task.

keyword.description: Search keyword identifying the local establishment; URL-encode it and keep it within the endpoint's character limit. Required when neither `cid` nor `place_id` is supplied.

cid.description: Google-defined unique ID of the business entity. Required when neither `keyword` nor `place_id` is supplied.

place_id.description: Google Maps Place ID of the business entity. Required when neither `keyword` nor `cid` is supplied.

priority.description: Task execution priority: `1` for normal priority, `2` for high priority (charged at a higher rate for faster handling).

location_name.description: Full location name (e.g. `London,England,United Kingdom`) setting the geographic context. Supply this or `location_code`/`location_coordinate`; valid values come from the matching Locations endpoint.

location_code.description: Numeric location code setting the geographic context. Supply this or `location_name`/`location_coordinate`; valid values come from the matching Locations endpoint.

location_coordinate.description: GPS coordinates in `latitude,longitude` (optionally with a radius) setting the geographic context. Supply this or `location_name`/`location_code`.

language_name.description: Full language name (e.g. `English`) for the search. Supply this or `language_code`; valid values come from the matching Languages endpoint.

language_code.description: Language code (e.g. `en`) for the search. Supply this or `language_name`; valid values come from the matching Languages endpoint.

depth.description: Parsing depth: number of reviews to collect from the SERP. Set it in multiples of 20, since results are processed twenty at a time.

tag.description: Your own task label (up to 255 characters), echoed back in the response `data` so you can correlate results with requests.

postback_url.description: URL to which finished task results are POSTed (gzip-compressed). Use `$id`/`$tag` placeholders to have them substituted automatically.

pingback_url.description: URL pinged via GET when the task completes, signalling that results are ready to collect. Supports `$id`/`$tag` placeholders.

get.summary: Get Google Extended Reviews Completed Tasks
get.description: List Google Extended Reviews tasks that have finished processing and are ready to be collected via the matching task_get endpoint.

version.description: Top-level API metadata object describing this response, including the API version and the aggregated status, timing and cost of the request.

version.status_code.description: Overall status code for the whole request. Use it as the first signal of success or failure before reading the tasks array; the full code list is in the DataForSEO errors reference.

version.status_message.description: Human-readable message that accompanies the overall status code, e.g. `Ok` or an error explanation.

version.time.description: Wall-clock time the platform spent handling the whole request, in seconds.

version.cost.description: Total amount charged for all tasks in this response, in USD.

version.tasks_count.description: Number of task objects contained in the `tasks` array.

version.tasks_error.description: Number of task objects in the `tasks` array that finished with an error; if greater than zero, inspect each task's own status code.

tasks.description: Array of task objects; each entry carries the per-task status plus its `result` payload.

tasks.id.description: Unique identifier of this task in UUID format. Reuse it to fetch results from the matching `task_get` endpoint within the task's retention window.

tasks.status_code.description: Status code of this individual task (range 10000-60000). A value in the 20000 range means success; other ranges signal queued, in-progress or error states.

tasks.status_message.description: Human-readable message describing the outcome of this task, paired with `tasks.status_code`.

tasks.time.description: Wall-clock time spent processing this task, in seconds.

tasks.cost.description: Amount charged for this individual task, in USD.

tasks.result_count.description: Number of elements in this task's `result` array.

tasks.path.description: URL path segments of the endpoint that produced this task, useful for tracing which call the result belongs to.

tasks.data.description: Echo of the parameters you submitted for this task, returned so you can correlate the result with the original request.

result.description: Array holding the actual payload of this task.

result.id.description: UUID of a completed task that is now ready to be collected; pass it to the matching `task_get` endpoint to download the results.

result.se.description: Search engine the completed task was set for.

result.se_type.description: Search engine type the completed task was set for.

result.date_posted.description: Timestamp (UTC) when the task was originally submitted.

result.tag.description: User-defined label that was attached to the task when it was created, echoed back for matching.

result.endpoint.description: Relative endpoint URL to call for collecting this completed task's results.

result.hotel_identifier.description: Unique Google identifier of a hotel entity; pass it as `hotel_identifier` to the Hotel Info endpoint.

result.location_code.description: Location code echoed from the POST request, identifying the geographic target of the search.

result.language_code.description: Language code echoed from the POST request, identifying the interface language of the search.

result.datetime.description: UTC timestamp of when this result was collected, format `yyyy-mm-dd hh-mm-ss +00:00`.

result.address.description: Street address of the business entity.

result.phone.description: Phone number of the business entity.

result.about.description: Object holding descriptive information about the hotel.

result.description.description: Description of the business entity shown in the SERP.

result.sub_descriptions.description: Additional descriptive details supplementing the hotel description.

result.full_address.description: Full address of the entity in standardized format.

result.url.description: Absolute URL associated with this element.

result.category.description: Primary Google My Business category that best describes the entity's services.

result.category_label.description: Display label of the amenity category.

result.items.description: Array of nested item elements belonging to this entry.

result.amenity_label.description: Display name of the amenity as shown to users.

result.hint.description: Standardized detail note about the amenity.

result.hint_label.description: Display detail note about the amenity.

result.is_available.description: Whether the amenity is available at the hotel.

result.location.description: Address of the establishment this result was collected for.

result.neighborhood.description: Name of the neighborhood the hotel is located in.

result.neighborhood_description.description: Free-text description of the hotel's neighborhood.

result.maps_url.description: URL to the entity's location on Google Maps.

result.overall_score.description: Overall location score from 1 to 5, blending proximity to things to do, restaurants, transit and airports (criteria not equally weighted).

result.score_by_categories.description: Per-category location scores (things to do, restaurants, transit, airports).

result.things_to_do.description: Location score (1-5) for proximity to nearby things to do.

result.restaurants.description: Location score (1-5) for proximity to nearby restaurants.

result.transit.description: Location score (1-5) for proximity to transit options.

result.airport_access.description: Location score (1-5) for proximity to airports.

result.latitude.description: Latitude coordinate of the entity on Google Maps.

result.longitude.description: Longitude coordinate of the entity on Google Maps.

result.location_chain.description: Elements of the hotel's location chain with their parameters.

result.card_id.description: Identifier of the hotel card element.

result.feature_id.description: Unique identifier of the SERP feature / business element returned for this result.

result.cid.description: Google-defined client ID of the local establishment; can be reused with Google reviews endpoints to pull full review data.

result.reviews.description: Object with hotel review and rating information, broken down by criteria.

result.value.description: Numeric rating value, interpreted according to `rating_type`.

result.votes_count.description: Number of votes / reviews the rating is based on.

result.mentions.description: Hotel review mentions broken down by criterion.

result.positive_count.description: Number of positive reviews for the criterion.

result.negative_count.description: Number of negative reviews for the criterion.

result.total_count.description: Total number of matching results available in our database for your request.

result.visible_by_default.description: Whether the review element is shown by default.

result.rating_distribution.description: Object breaking down vote counts per star level (1 through 5).

result.rating.description: Rating object summarizing review-based popularity of the entity.

result.rating_type.description: Rating scale in use; values include `Max5`, `Percents`, `CustomMax`, indicating how `value` should be interpreted.

result.rating_max.description: Maximum possible value for the given `rating_type`.

result.overview_images.description: URLs of images shown in the hotel overview.

result.prices.description: Pricing details for the hotel stay.

result.price_without_discount.description: Price per night before any discount is applied.

result.check_in.description: Check-in date-time in UTC, format `yyyy-mm-dd hh-mm-ss +00:00`.

result.check_out.description: Check-out date-time in UTC, format `yyyy-mm-dd hh-mm-ss +00:00`.

result.visitors.description: Number of visitors the price applies to.

result.type.description: Element type tag of this result entry.

result.is_paid.description: Whether this is a paid listing/ad (`true`) versus an organic/free booking link (`false`).

result.free_cancellation_until.description: UTC date until which free cancellation is available; `null` if free cancellation is not offered.

result.max_visitors.description: Maximum number of visitors the price offer is valid for.

result.offer_images.description: URLs of images featured in the price offer.

result.prices_by_dates.description: Array of hotel stay prices broken down by date.

hotel_identifier.description: Unique Google identifier of a hotel entity (required). Obtain it from the advanced Google SERP API or from Hotel Searches results.

location_name.description: Full location name (e.g. `London,England,United Kingdom`) setting the geographic context. Supply this or `location_code`/`location_coordinate`; valid values come from the matching Locations endpoint.

location_code.description: Numeric location code setting the geographic context. Supply this or `location_name`/`location_coordinate`; valid values come from the matching Locations endpoint.

location_coordinate.description: GPS coordinates in `latitude,longitude` (optionally with a radius) setting the geographic context. Supply this or `location_name`/`location_code`.

language_name.description: Full language name (e.g. `English`) for the search. Supply this or `language_code`; valid values come from the matching Languages endpoint.

language_code.description: Language code (e.g. `en`) for the search. Supply this or `language_name`; valid values come from the matching Languages endpoint.

check_in.description: Check-in date in `yyyy-mm-dd`; must not be earlier than today. Defaults to tomorrow when omitted.

check_out.description: Check-out date in `yyyy-mm-dd`; must be later than `check_in`. Defaults to two days from now when omitted.

currency.description: Currency for the returned prices, e.g. `USD`.

adults.description: Number of adult guests. Defaults to two adults when omitted.

children.description: Children to include, given as an array of their ages (e.g. `[14]`). Defaults to no children when omitted.

load_prices_by_dates.description: Set to `true` to include the `prices_by_dates` array with stay prices split by date in the response.

prices_start_date.description: Start date (`yyyy-mm-dd`) for date-split prices; requires `load_prices_by_dates=true`. Defaults to `check_in`.

prices_end_date.description: End date (`yyyy-mm-dd`) for date-split prices; requires `load_prices_by_dates=true`. Defaults to one month of prices.

prices_date_range.description: Predefined period for date-split prices (alternative to start/end dates); requires `load_prices_by_dates=true`.

tasks.description: List of task objects, one per posted task, each holding its own status and `result` payload.

tasks.id.description: Unique task identifier (UUID). Reuse it on the matching `task_get` endpoint to fetch this task's results within the retention window.

tasks.status_code.description: Per-task status code in the 10000-60000 range; `20000` means the task completed successfully while other values signal task-level errors.

tasks.status_message.description: Human-readable message explaining this task's `status_code`.

tasks.time.description: Processing time of this individual task, in seconds.

tasks.cost.description: Money charged for this individual task, in US dollars.

tasks.result_count.description: Number of elements in this task's `result` array.

tasks.path.description: URL path segments of the endpoint that produced this task, useful for tracing which call the task belongs to.

tasks.data.description: Echo of the parameters you supplied when posting the task, returned so you can correlate results with your input.

result.description: Array holding the actual data payload of the task.

hotel_identifier.description: Unique Google identifier of a hotel entity (required). Obtain it from the advanced Google SERP API or from Hotel Searches results.

keyword.description: Search keyword identifying the local establishment; URL-encode it and keep it within the endpoint's character limit. Required when neither `cid` nor `place_id` is supplied.

priority.description: Task execution priority: `1` for normal priority, `2` for high priority (charged at a higher rate for faster handling).

location_name.description: Full location name (e.g. `London,England,United Kingdom`) setting the geographic context. Supply this or `location_code`/`location_coordinate`; valid values come from the matching Locations endpoint.

location_code.description: Numeric location code setting the geographic context. Supply this or `location_name`/`location_coordinate`; valid values come from the matching Locations endpoint.

location_coordinate.description: GPS coordinates in `latitude,longitude` (optionally with a radius) setting the geographic context. Supply this or `location_name`/`location_code`.

language_name.description: Full language name (e.g. `English`) for the search. Supply this or `language_code`; valid values come from the matching Languages endpoint.

language_code.description: Language code (e.g. `en`) for the search. Supply this or `language_name`; valid values come from the matching Languages endpoint.

check_in.description: Check-in date in `yyyy-mm-dd`; must not be earlier than today. Defaults to tomorrow when omitted.

check_out.description: Check-out date in `yyyy-mm-dd`; must be later than `check_in`. Defaults to two days from now when omitted.

currency.description: Currency for the returned prices, e.g. `USD`.

adults.description: Number of adult guests. Defaults to two adults when omitted.

children.description: Children to include, given as an array of their ages (e.g. `[14]`). Defaults to no children when omitted.

load_prices_by_dates.description: Set to `true` to include the `prices_by_dates` array with stay prices split by date in the response.

prices_start_date.description: Start date (`yyyy-mm-dd`) for date-split prices; requires `load_prices_by_dates=true`. Defaults to `check_in`.

prices_end_date.description: End date (`yyyy-mm-dd`) for date-split prices; requires `load_prices_by_dates=true`. Defaults to one month of prices.

prices_date_range.description: Predefined period for date-split prices (alternative to start/end dates); requires `load_prices_by_dates=true`.

tag.description: Your own task label (up to 255 characters), echoed back in the response `data` so you can correlate results with requests.

postback_url.description: URL to which finished task results are POSTed (gzip-compressed). Use `$id`/`$tag` placeholders to have them substituted automatically.

postback_data.description: Datatype pushed to your `postback_url`; required when `postback_url` is set. Possible values: `advanced`, `html`.

pingback_url.description: URL pinged via GET when the task completes, signalling that results are ready to collect. Supports `$id`/`$tag` placeholders.

keyword.description: Name of the local establishment to look up (up to 700 characters); percent-encoding is decoded. This field can also carry a Google-defined `cid` to target a specific establishment.

priority.description: Execution priority of the task: `1` runs at normal priority, `2` runs at high priority for faster turnaround at an extra charge.

depth.description: Number of updates to parse. Best set in multiples of ten since updates are processed ten at a time; Google returns at most 4490 updates.

tag.description: Your own label for the task (up to 255 characters). It is echoed back in the response `data` so you can match results to requests.

postback_url.description: URL to which the completed task results are pushed via POST (gzip-compressed). Use the `$id`/`$tag` placeholders in the URL to have them substituted automatically.

pingback_url.description: URL that is pinged via GET when the task completes, signalling that results are ready to collect. Supports the `$id`/`$tag` placeholders.

tasks.status_code.description: Per-task status code in the 10000-60000 range; `20000` means the task completed successfully while other values signal task-level errors.

tasks.status_message.description: Human-readable message explaining this task's `status_code`.

tasks.time.description: Processing time of this individual task, in seconds.

tasks.cost.description: Money charged for this individual task, in US dollars.

tasks.result_count.description: Number of elements in this task's `result` array.

tasks.path.description: URL path segments of the endpoint that produced this task, useful for tracing which call the task belongs to.

tasks.data.description: Echo of the parameters you supplied when posting the task, returned so you can correlate results with your input.

result.description: Array holding the actual data payload of the task.

keyword.description: Search keyword identifying the local establishment; URL-encode it and keep it within the endpoint's character limit. Required when neither `cid` nor `place_id` is supplied.

cid.description: Google-defined unique ID of the business entity. Required when neither `keyword` nor `place_id` is supplied.

place_id.description: Google Maps Place ID of the business entity. Required when neither `keyword` nor `cid` is supplied.

priority.description: Task execution priority: `1` for normal priority, `2` for high priority (charged at a higher rate for faster handling).

location_name.description: Full location name (e.g. `London,England,United Kingdom`) setting the geographic context. Supply this or `location_code`/`location_coordinate`; valid values come from the matching Locations endpoint.

location_code.description: Numeric location code setting the geographic context. Supply this or `location_name`/`location_coordinate`; valid values come from the matching Locations endpoint.

location_coordinate.description: GPS coordinates in `latitude,longitude` (optionally with a radius) setting the geographic context. Supply this or `location_name`/`location_code`.

language_name.description: Full language name (e.g. `English`) for the search. Supply this or `language_code`; valid values come from the matching Languages endpoint.

language_code.description: Language code (e.g. `en`) for the search. Supply this or `language_name`; valid values come from the matching Languages endpoint.

depth.description: Parsing depth: number of reviews to collect from the SERP. Set it in multiples of 20, since results are processed twenty at a time.

sort_by.description: Sorting of hotel-search results; values include `relevance`, `lowest_price`, `highest_rating`. For reviews, sorts review order.

tag.description: Your own task label (up to 255 characters), echoed back in the response `data` so you can correlate results with requests.

postback_url.description: URL to which finished task results are POSTed (gzip-compressed). Use `$id`/`$tag` placeholders to have them substituted automatically.

pingback_url.description: URL pinged via GET when the task completes, signalling that results are ready to collect. Supports `$id`/`$tag` placeholders.

post.summary: Setting Business Data Tripadvisor Reviews Tasks
post.description: Create an asynchronous task that collects Tripadvisor reviews for a business identified by URL path or keyword, with optional filters by rating, traveler type, visit month and review keyword.

version.description: Top-level API metadata object describing this response, including the API version and the aggregated status, timing and cost of the request.

version.status_code.description: Overall status code for the whole request. Use it as the first signal of success or failure before reading the tasks array; the full code list is in the DataForSEO errors reference.

version.status_message.description: Human-readable message that accompanies the overall status code, e.g. `Ok` or an error explanation.

version.time.description: Wall-clock time the platform spent handling the whole request, in seconds.

version.cost.description: Total amount charged for all tasks in this response, in USD.

version.tasks_count.description: Number of task objects contained in the `tasks` array.

version.tasks_error.description: Number of task objects in the `tasks` array that finished with an error; if greater than zero, inspect each task's own status code.

tasks.description: Array of task objects; each entry carries the per-task status plus its `result` payload.

tasks.id.description: Unique identifier of this task in UUID format. Reuse it to fetch results from the matching `task_get` endpoint within the task's retention window.

tasks.status_code.description: Status code of this individual task (range 10000-60000). A value in the 20000 range means success; other ranges signal queued, in-progress or error states.

tasks.status_message.description: Human-readable message describing the outcome of this task, paired with `tasks.status_code`.

tasks.time.description: Wall-clock time spent processing this task, in seconds.

tasks.cost.description: Amount charged for this individual task, in USD.

tasks.result_count.description: Number of elements in this task's `result` array.

tasks.path.description: URL path segments of the endpoint that produced this task, useful for tracing which call the result belongs to.

tasks.data.description: Echo of the parameters you submitted for this task, returned so you can correlate the result with the original request.

result.description: Result array; for this task-creation response the value is `null`.

url_path.description: Tripadvisor page URL path of the business entity. Provide this or `keyword`. You can obtain it from the Tripadvisor Search items' `url_path`.

keyword.description: Name of an existing Tripadvisor business or place (up to 700 characters; percent-encoding decoded). Provide this or `url_path`.

location_name.description: Full location name; required if you specify neither `location_code` nor `url_path`. The available list comes from the Tripadvisor `/locations` endpoint.

location_code.description: Numeric location code; required if you specify neither `location_name` nor `url_path`. The available list comes from the Tripadvisor `/locations` endpoint.

priority.description: Execution priority of the task: `1` runs at normal priority, `2` runs at high priority for faster turnaround at an extra charge.

language_name.description: Full language name. Using this field incurs one extra request charge. The available list comes from the Tripadvisor `/languages` endpoint.

language_code.description: Language code. Using this field incurs one extra request charge. The available list comes from the Tripadvisor `/languages` endpoint.

depth.description: Number of reviews to parse; best set in multiples of ten (processed ten at a time). Default 10, max 4490. Billing is per SERP of up to 10 results.

ratings.description: Filter reviews by Tripadvisor traveler rating; business meaning of values: `excellent`, `very_good`, `average`, `poor`, `terrible`. Multiple values allowed.

visit_type.description: Filter by traveler type; business meaning of values: `families`, `couples`, `solo`, `business`, `friends`. Multiple values allowed.

months.description: Filter by the month of the traveler's visit, given as month names (e.g. `january`). Multiple values allowed.

search_reviews_keyword.description: Return only reviews that contain the specified keyword.

sort_by.description: Sort order of the returned reviews; business meaning: `most_recent` newest first, `detailed_reviews` most detailed first.

translate_reviews.description: When `true`, reviews are translated to the language matching the `url_path` (e.g. Italian for a `tripadvisor.it` path).

tag.description: Your own label for the task (up to 255 characters). It is echoed back in the response `data` so you can match results to requests.

postback_url.description: URL to which the completed task results are pushed via POST (gzip-compressed). Use the `$id`/`$tag` placeholders in the URL to have them substituted automatically.

pingback_url.description: URL that is pinged via GET when the task completes, signalling that results are ready to collect. Supports the `$id`/`$tag` placeholders.

post.description: Queue a Google Organic SERP task for a keyword/location/language; you are charged only for posting, and up to 100 tasks can be sent per call. The response returns a task id used to collect the SERP later via the matching task_get endpoint or via postback/pingback.

version.description: API version string that produced this response.

status_code.description: Overall response status code; check it before reading the payload.

status_message.description: Human-readable message accompanying the overall status code.

time.description: Server-side execution time of the request, in seconds.

cost.description: Total billed cost of this response across all tasks, in USD.

tasks_count.description: Number of task entries contained in the `tasks` array.

tasks_error.description: How many tasks finished with an error; if non-zero, inspect each task's own status fields.

tasks[].status_code.description: Per-task status code; check it to tell whether this individual task succeeded.

tasks[].cost.description: Billed cost attributed to this task, in USD.

tasks[].result_count.description: Number of entries in this task's `result` array.

keyword.description: Search term to query. URL-encode it; the SERP is built around this query.

location_name.description: Full text name of the search-engine location (e.g. `London,England,United Kingdom`); used to localize the SERP.

location_code.description: Numeric code of the search-engine location; the code-based alternative to `location_name`, obtainable from the locations reference endpoint.

location_coordinate.description: GPS coordinates of the target location, as `latitude,longitude`; used for fine-grained geo-targeting.

language_name.description: Full text name of the search-engine language (e.g. `English`); the SERP is returned in this language.

language_code.description: Two-letter language code (e.g. `en`); the code-based alternative to `language_name`.

depth.description: Number of SERP results to parse; deeper values cost more crawl pages and increase task price.

max_crawl_pages.description: Upper bound on the number of SERP pages to crawl for this task.

device.description: Device profile to emulate for the search, such as `desktop` or `mobile`; affects layout and result set.

os.description: Operating system to emulate for the chosen device, refining the user-agent presented to Google.

group_organic_results.description: When enabled, related organic results are merged and reported as grouped organic entries instead of separate items.

calculate_rectangles.description: When enabled, pixel-coordinate rankings (rectangles) are computed for elements in the advanced SERP, useful for visual position analysis.

postback_url.description: Your endpoint that DataForSEO calls with the completed task results once the task finishes.

pingback_url.description: Your endpoint that DataForSEO pings to notify you that the task is complete, without sending the payload.

postback_data.description: Result format delivered to `postback_url`; choose the variant (such as advanced or html) matching how you want results pushed.

tag.description: Your own identifier echoed back with the task, useful for correlating results with your records.

tasks[].result.description: Result payload array for this task; element shape depends on the endpoint (SERP items, task references, locations, languages, etc.).

url.description: Direct Google search URL to parse instead of building the query from keyword/location/language parameters. Use one or the other.

location_name.description: Full location name to target, e.g. `London,England,United Kingdom`. Provide either `location_name`, `location_code` or `location_coordinate`.

location_code.description: Numeric location code to target; obtainable from the matching `.../locations` endpoint. Provide either `location_name`, `location_code` or `location_coordinate`.

location_coordinate.description: GPS coordinates to target, formatted as `latitude,longitude` (optionally with radius). Alternative to `location_name`/`location_code`.

language_name.description: Full language name to use, e.g. `English`. Provide either `language_name` or `language_code`.

language_code.description: Language code to use, e.g. `en`; obtainable from the matching `.../languages` endpoint. Provide either `language_name` or `language_code`.

device.description: Device the SERP is emulated on, typically `desktop` or `mobile`.

os.description: Operating system to emulate for the chosen device, e.g. `windows`, `macos`, `android`, `ios`.

se_domain.description: Custom Google domain to query, e.g. `google.co.uk`, overriding the domain inferred from location.

depth.description: Number of SERP results to parse. Larger depth returns more results and may cost more.

max_crawl_pages.description: Maximum number of SERP pages to crawl for this query.

search_param.description: Additional raw Google search parameters appended to the query (e.g. URL query-string fragments).

load_async_ai_overview.description: When true, additionally loads the asynchronously-rendered AI Overview block of the SERP.

expand_ai_overview.description: When true, expands the AI Overview item to capture its full expanded content.

tag.description: User-defined label stored with the task and echoed back in results, for your own correlation.

status_code.description: Overall status code for the whole response. `20000` means the call succeeded; any other value signals a request-level failure (auth, quota, malformed payload) — see `status_message`.

status_message.description: Human-readable text for `status_code`: `Ok.` on success, or the failure reason otherwise.

time.description: Total server-side processing time for the response, as a duration string such as `0.1234 sec`.

cost.description: Total amount charged for this request in USD, aggregated over all tasks in the response.

tasks_count.description: Number of task objects in the `tasks` array.

tasks_error.description: Number of tasks that finished with an error; `0` means all tasks succeeded.

tasks[].cost.description: Amount charged for this individual task, in USD.

tasks[].result_count.description: Number of elements in this task's `result` array.

keyword.description: Search term to query. URL-encode it; the SERP is built around this query.

depth.description: Number of SERP results to parse; deeper values cost more crawl pages and increase task price.

keyword.description: Search query to run on the search engine, exactly as a user would type it. This is the only required field for keyword-based endpoints.

language_code.description: Language code for the search; obtain valid values from the engine's languages endpoint. Provide either this or `language_name`.

device.description: Device profile to emulate for the search, which can change how results are ranked and rendered.

os.description: Operating system to emulate together with `device`.
